name: Numbers
description: Questions on int, float, and numeric operations.

questions.question: In Python 3, 3 / 2 equals:
questions.explanation: In Python 3, / is true division and returns a float.

questions.question: Which can represent arbitrarily large integers?
questions.explanation: Python's int has arbitrary precision; float has limited range and precision.

questions.question: 0.1 + 0.2 == 0.3 in Python typically evaluates to:
questions.explanation: Floats are binary; 0.1 and 0.2 are not exact, so 0.1 + 0.2 may not equal 0.3 exactly.

questions.question: For financial calculations, a better choice than float is often:
questions.explanation: decimal.Decimal provides decimal floating point and avoids float representation errors.
